mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which compresses aggregate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where inflation exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, which translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict forward return direction with an accuracy rate exceeding 52 percent across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side disruption and falling factory utilization, which triggers risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases during contraction phases.
